Package i5.cb.graph.diagram

Class DiagramGlassPane

  • java.lang.Object
    • java.awt.Component
      • java.awt.Container
        • javax.swing.JComponent
          • i5.cb.graph.diagram.DiagramGlassPane

  • public class DiagramGlassPane
extends javax.swing.JComponent
    That's a glasspane supposed to be every DiagramNode's standard glasspane. It may be modified to make not the whole area of the node sensitive for example or to paint certain symbols over parts of the DiagramNode. The GlassPane will be added to the PopupLayer of the DiagramDesktop so it is above the DiagramObjects. Then a DiagramNodeMouseListener will be added as the glasspane's mouselistener
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

      • getBounds

        public java.awt.Rectangle getBounds​()
        Gets this glassPane boundaries. This method is somehow needed to correctly paint the DiagramNode, but we don't understand yet, how.
        Overrides:
        getBounds in class java.awt.Component
        Returns:
        the bounding rectangel of this glasspane

      • contains

        public boolean contains​(int x,
                        int y)
        If the parent DiagramNode is represented by a small Component, the GlassPane covers the whole Node. Else it covers a smaller Rectangle defined by inner bounds}.
        Overrides:
        contains in class javax.swing.JComponent
        Parameters:
        x - horizontal coordinate relative to this GlassPane's(its Node's) position
        y - vertical coordinate relative to this GlassPane's(its Node's) position
        Returns:
        true, iff the given point is inside the defined GlassPane's Bounds
